## Worth mentioning

### 1. I found 10k GitHub repositories distributing Trojan malware

- Source: [orchidfiles.com](https://orchidfiles.com/github-repositories-distributing-malware/)
- Category: AI Operations / Agent Control
- Published: 2026-06-20T00:00:00.000Z
- Labels: reproducible, publish_public
- Product relevance: none

Source facts:
- An independent investigation identified roughly 10,000 GitHub repositories distributing Trojan malware, undetected for over a year.

Builder/operator takeaway: Directly actionable: vet provenance before cloning or running random GitHub repos, tools, or MCP servers — a routine part of a solo builder's workflow.

Uncertainty: GitHub's official response/remediation status wasn't confirmed at time of writing; scope of true victim count is researcher-estimated.

### 2. Google Workspace threatening to block Firefox access

- Source: [tales.fromprod.com](https://tales.fromprod.com/2026/169/google-workspace-threatening-to-block-firefox.html)
- Category: Model + API Changes
- Published: 2026-06-20T00:00:00.000Z
- Labels: builder_reported, publish_public
- Product relevance: none

Source facts:
- Some Google Workspace accounts are blocking or warning Firefox users to switch to Chrome, apparently tied to org-level device-compliance security settings.

Builder/operator takeaway: A platform forcing browser choice on Workspace accounts is a real, immediate decision point for anyone using Firefox with a managed Google account.

Uncertainty: Not yet confirmed whether this is a deliberate Google-wide policy, an opt-in admin security feature, or a rollout bug — reporting suggests the latter two are more likely than a systematic Firefox block.

### 3. Zero-Touch OAuth for MCP

- Source: [blog.modelcontextprotocol.io](https://blog.modelcontextprotocol.io/posts/enterprise-managed-auth/)
- Category: Model + API Changes
- Published: 2026-06-20T00:00:00.000Z
- Labels: production_proven, publish_public
- Product relevance: none

Source facts:
- MCP's Enterprise-Managed Authorization ('Zero-Touch OAuth') extension reached stable status on June 18, 2026, and is being adopted by Anthropic, Microsoft, and Okta.

Builder/operator takeaway: A first-party protocol-level change directly relevant to anyone building or deploying MCP servers — squarely in scope for ongoing MCP/agent work.

Uncertainty: Adoption is just starting; not yet clear how quickly the broader MCP client/host ecosystem (beyond the named early adopters) will implement it.

### 4. Datasette Apps: Host custom HTML applications inside Datasette

- Source: [simonwillison.net](https://simonwillison.net/2026/Jun/18/datasette-apps/)
- Category: Tools Worth Testing
- Published: 2026-06-20T00:00:00.000Z
- Labels: reproducible, publish_private
- Product relevance: none

Source facts:
- Datasette Apps lets you host custom HTML applications directly inside a Datasette instance.

Builder/operator takeaway: Concrete, usable feature for Datasette users wanting lightweight internal tools without separate hosting.

Uncertainty: Still alpha (pre-1.0 plugin versions), so API and stability may shift before general release.

### 5. The design of littlefs

- Source: [github.com](https://github.com/littlefs-project/littlefs/blob/master/DESIGN.md)
- Category: Data Infrastructure / Verification / Scraping
- Published: 2026-06-20T00:00:00.000Z
- Labels: production_proven, publish_private
- Product relevance: none

Source facts:
- littlefs's design document explains how the embedded filesystem achieves power-loss resilience and wear leveling with bounded RAM use.

Builder/operator takeaway: High-quality technical reference for embedded-systems work, though narrow in scope.
